part_title: Section Eighteen: Applied Programmes for Managing Tuta absoluta Under Egyptian Conditions
part_short_title: Applied Programmes for Managing Tuta absoluta Under Egyptian Conditions
part_subtitle: Applied IPM Programmes for Tuta absoluta Under Egyptian Conditions

items.text: Chapter Ten
Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Introduction

Tomato production in Egypt is characterized by a wide range of production regions, variable temperatures, overlapping planting dates, and the prolonged presence of solanaceous hosts during substantial parts of the year. It is therefore not possible to develop one national programme for managing Tuta absoluta and apply it identically across all governorates and production cycles.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tomato production in Egypt is characterized by a wide range of production regions, variable temperatures, overlapping planting dates, and the prolonged presence of [[term:solanaceae]]solanaceous hosts[[/term]] during substantial parts of the year. It is therefore not possible to develop one national programme for managing Tuta absoluta and apply it identically across all governorates and production cycles.

items.text: A programme suitable for a long-cycle processing-tomato field in newly reclaimed land may not be suitable for a short-cycle fresh-market tomato field in the Nile Delta. Likewise, a programme that performs well during a relatively cool period may fail during a period when pest development accelerates.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A programme suitable for a long-cycle processing-tomato field in newly reclaimed land may not be suitable for a short-cycle fresh-market tomato field in the Nile Delta. Likewise, a programme that performs well during a relatively cool period may fail during a period when pest development accelerates.

items.text: The Egyptian programme is influenced by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The Egyptian programme is influenced by:

items: Overlap of tomato with potato and eggplant.
Presence of naturally occurring or released natural enemies.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Overlap of tomato with potato and eggplant.
[[prepared]]Presence of naturally occurring or released [[term:biological-control]]natural enemies[[/term]].

items.text: The technically correct question is therefore not:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The technically correct question is therefore not:

items.text: What is the Tuta absoluta programme for Egypt?
But:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]But:

items.text: What is the appropriate programme for this field, in this region, in this production cycle, under the current risk level?
Scientific Basis for Regional Differentiation
The development rate of Tuta absoluta changes with temperature, and therefore the following also vary:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The development rate of Tuta absoluta changes with temperature, and therefore the following also vary:

items: Rate of progression through larval instars.
Number of generations that may overlap with the crop cycle.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Number of generations that may overlap with the crop cycle.

items.text: Egyptian regions also differ in:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Egyptian regions also differ in:

items.text: Egyptian studies have shown that pest and natural-enemy population dynamics are not constant among locations or periods. Thermal models have also shown that expected generation number and adult-emergence timing differ among Giza, Qena, and Marsa Matrouh.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Egyptian studies have shown that pest and natural-enemy population dynamics are not constant among locations or periods. Thermal models have also shown that expected generation number and adult-emergence timing differ among Giza, Qena, and Marsa Matrouh.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following should therefore be used:

items.text: Rather than relying on one national calendar.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rather than relying on one national calendar.

items.text: Programme One
Open-Field Processing Tomatoes
Characteristics of Processing Tomatoes Affecting the Programme
Processing tomatoes are often characterized by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Processing tomatoes are often characterized by:

items: One harvest or a limited number of pickings.
Importance of canopy cover for fruit protection.
Transport of large quantities over a short period.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]One harvest or a limited number of pickings.
[[prepared]]Importance of canopy cover for fruit protection.
[[prepared]]Transport of large quantities over a short period.

items.text: The Tuta programme in processing tomatoes should achieve four objectives:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The Tuta programme in processing tomatoes should achieve four objectives:

items: 1. Maintain a functional canopy until harvest approaches.
3. Comply with factory lists and residue requirements.
4. Terminate the field immediately after delivery ends.

[[prepared]]3. Comply with factory lists and residue requirements.

Field termination should be completed within a short period after final delivery through:

These are often characterized by:

A common error is extending the field to obtain a few low-return pickings while pest pressure increases.

items.text: Programme Three
Winter or Relatively Cool Production Cycle
Risk Characteristics Under Cool Conditions
Relatively cool conditions may cause:

items: Reduced flight activity during some periods.
Slower expression of some biological-control effects.

These include:

Leaving mines unexamined because they develop slowly.
Delayed ripening and extended crop duration.
Overlap with a following spring crop.
Repeated insecticide use because visible damage persists.

items.text: Programme Four
Transitional Spring Production Cycle
Characteristics of the Spring Cycle
The spring period is often characterized by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The spring period is often characterized by:

items: Overlap of old winter fields with new plantings.
Increasing suitability for natural enemies and some microbial agents.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Overlap of old winter fields with new plantings.
[[prepared]]Increasing suitability for natural enemies and some microbial agents.

items.text: It is one of the periods requiring the closest monitoring because pest-development speed may change within only a few weeks.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It is one of the periods requiring the closest monitoring because pest-development speed may change within only a few weeks.

items.text: Risks in the Spring Cycle
These include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These include:

items.text: The programme should not rely on the previous week's reading after a clear temperature increase or removal of a neighbouring field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme should not rely on the previous week's reading after a clear temperature increase or removal of a neighbouring field.

items.text: Priorities of the Spring Programme

items: 1. Shorten the inspection interval as temperatures rise.
2. Use traps together with edge monitoring.
3. Link trap data to old-field removal dates.
6. Use Bt and early-stage agents at the correct time.
7. Recalibrate the sprayer as plants grow rapidly.
8. Avoid broad-spectrum insecticides that kill beneficial organisms.
9. Prepare the resistance-management plan before fruiting.
10. Monitor the beginning of cluster and fruit infestation.

items.text: Management of Adjacent Winter Fields
When a winter field is close to removal:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When a winter field is close to removal:

items: Determine the date of final harvest.
Monitor traps between the two fields.
Increase inspection of the facing edge.
Do not apply preventive sprays without evidence of oviposition.

items.text: Programme Five
Summer Production Cycle or Hot Conditions
Risk Characteristics Under High Temperatures
Temperatures within the biologically suitable range for the pest may cause:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Temperatures within the biologically suitable range for the pest may cause:

items: Rapid progression of infestation into growing points and fruits.

items: Actual plant-surface temperature differs from weather-station temperature.

These include:

Minimizing the interval between receipt and distribution.
Providing a biological belt or suitable resources.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Minimizing the interval between receipt and distribution.
[[prepared]]Providing a biological belt or suitable resources.

items.text: Release rates from a temperate environment should not be transferred without local calibration.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Release rates from a temperate environment should not be transferred without local calibration.

items.text: Programme Eleven
Sinai and Similar Desert Regions
General Characteristics
These regions may be characterized by:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These regions may be characterized by:

items: Greater dependence on transplants from other regions.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Greater dependence on transplants from other regions.

items.text: Preventing pest entry with transplants is therefore critical.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Preventing pest entry with transplants is therefore critical.

items.text: Priorities of the Desert Programme

items: 3. Maintain a record for every transplant source.
6. Use vegetation margins without excessive water consumption.
9. Prepare a contingency plan for delayed supplies.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]3. Maintain a record for every transplant source.
[[prepared]]6. Use vegetation margins without excessive water consumption.
[[prepared]]9. Prepare a contingency plan for delayed supplies.

items.text: Programme Twelve
Short-Cycle Production
Characteristics of a Short Cycle
These include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These include:

items: Limited time for pest establishment and multiplication.
Relatively fewer generations within the field.
High importance of preventing early infestation.

items.text: Infestation carried with transplants may eliminate the advantage of a short cycle.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infestation carried with transplants may eliminate the advantage of a short cycle.

items.text: Priorities of a Short Cycle

items: 3. Conduct intensive inspection during the first weeks.
4. Prevent establishment of the first generation.
8. Protect fruits during the short risk period.
10. Terminate the field immediately after harvest.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]3. Conduct intensive inspection during the first weeks.
[[prepared]]4. Prevent establishment of the first generation.
[[prepared]]8. Protect fruits during the short risk period.
[[prepared]]10. Terminate the field immediately after harvest.

items.text: Selecting an Intervention in a Short Cycle
A slow-acting method may not provide a return when started too late, but may be effective when used preventively or early.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A slow-acting method may not provide a return when started too late, but may be effective when used preventively or early.

items.text: Before intervention, ask:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Before intervention, ask:

items: Is there sufficient time for its effect to appear?
Will it protect the fruits already present?
Is it compatible with harvest timing?
Is the cost justified by the remaining crop?
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Is there sufficient time for its effect to appear?
[[prepared]]Will it protect the fruits already present?
[[prepared]]Is the cost justified by the remaining crop?

items.text: Programme Thirteen
Long-Cycle Production
Characteristics of a Long Cycle
A long cycle presents a greater challenge because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A long cycle presents a greater challenge because of:

items: Need to conserve natural enemies for a long period.
Continued plant growth and canopy closure.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Need to conserve natural enemies for a long period.

items.text: A long cycle cannot be managed through insecticides alone without substantial selection pressure.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A long cycle cannot be managed through insecticides alone without substantial selection pressure.

items.text: Priorities of a Long Cycle

items: 1. Establish a biological programme from the beginning.
6. Divide the season into IRAC windows.
7. Record all treatments targeting other pests.
9. Prevent accumulation of advanced larval instars.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Establish a biological programme from the beginning.
[[prepared]]6. Divide the season into IRAC windows.
[[prepared]]7. Record all treatments targeting other pests.
[[prepared]]9. Prevent accumulation of advanced larval instars.

items.text: Resistance Management in a Long Cycle
The programme should:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The programme should:

items: Avoid repeating a group across successive generations.
Include periods based on non-chemical measures.
Avoid changing only the trade name.
Include sprays targeting whiteflies and thrips.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoid repeating a group across successive generations.

items.text: IRAC records become an operational necessity, not merely an administrative document.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]IRAC records become an operational necessity, not merely an administrative document.

items.text: Egyptian Programme According to External Pressure
Low External Pressure
Moderate External Pressure
High External Pressure
Active Immigration Wave

items: Use parasitoids or a hatching-stage intervention when eggs are present.
Do not spray the field from trap counts alone.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Use parasitoids or a hatching-stage intervention when eggs are present.
[[prepared]]Do not spray the field from trap counts alone.

items.text: Matrix for Selecting the Egyptian Programme

rows: Long-cycle processing tomatoes in newly reclaimed land
Sector-based monitoring + early biological programme + resistance windows + canopy and fruit protection
Frequent fruit inspection + PHI management + collection of infested fruit + hotspot interventions
Continued monitoring with temperature-adjusted assessment timing and preparation for spring increase
Intensified inspection as temperature rises and management of migration from old fields
Close inspection, rapid targeting of hatching, and protection of biological agents from heat
Prevention of early infestation and review of accumulated regional insecticide pressure
Neighbour coordination, edge inspection, and management of nurseries and residues
Sector division, hotspot maps, and centralized monitoring
Faster response, thermal monitoring, and suitable application timing
Interpretation of local wind and temperature without transferring inland calendars
Prevention through healthy transplants, early protection, timely harvest, and rapid termination
Continuous biological control, IRAC windows, and resistance monitoring

items.text: Flexible Weekly Programme Model
Each week, complete the following sequence.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each week, complete the following sequence.

items.text: First: Review Weather

items: Effects on eggs and biological agents.

items.text: Second: Review Traps
Third: Inspect Plants
Fourth: Evaluate Beneficial Organisms
Fifth: Review Surrounding Fields
Sixth: Select the Decision Level
Seventh: Define the Action
Elements That Should Be Excluded from Egyptian Programmes
Programmes should not become:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Programmes should not become:

items: One uniform mixture for all governorates.
A schedule that ignores crop stage.
A programme that neglects the nursery.
A programme beginning after fruit infestation appears.
A programme that does not record natural enemies.
A programme that does not review residues.
A programme that does not review resistance.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A programme beginning after fruit infestation appears.
[[prepared]]A programme that does not record natural enemies.
[[prepared]]A programme that does not review residues.
[[prepared]]A programme that does not review resistance.

items.text: Criteria for Approving Any Local Programme
A programme should not be approved unless it specifies:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A programme should not be approved unless it specifies:

items.text: Local Programme Documentation Template
Common Errors in Applying Regional Programmes
Error One: Using a Delta Programme in Upper Egypt
Temperature, development rate, and operational conditions differ.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Temperature, development rate, and operational conditions differ.

items.text: Error Two: Using a Winter Programme in Summer
Hatching time and reassessment intervals differ.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Hatching time and reassessment intervals differ.

items.text: Error Three: Assuming Processing Tomatoes Need Less Fruit Protection
Fruit infestation affects acceptance, quality, and rots.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fruit infestation affects acceptance, quality, and rots.

items.text: Error Four: Using the Same Programme for Short and Long Cycles
Generation number and resistance pressure differ.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Generation number and resistance pressure differ.

items.text: Error Five: Setting Rigid Monthly Dates
Weather and transplanting dates change among years.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Weather and transplanting dates change among years.

items.text: Error Six: Ignoring the Transplant Source
The field may begin with internal infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The field may begin with internal infestation.

items.text: Error Seven: Neglecting Old Fields
They may undermine the best programme in a new field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]They may undermine the best programme in a new field.

items.text: Error Eight: Using Foreign Release Rates Without Testing
Temperature, wind, strain, and production system differ.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Temperature, wind, strain, and production system differ.

items.text: Error Nine: Applying Fungi Under Unsuitable Conditions
This reduces viability and efficacy.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This reduces viability and efficacy.

items.text: Error Ten: Assuming Winter Is Risk-Free
A generation may persist for a longer period.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A generation may persist for a longer period.

items.text: Error Eleven: Assuming Extreme Heat Eliminates the Pest
Mines and fruits protect many larvae.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Mines and fruits protect many larvae.

items.text: Error Twelve: Failing to Adjust Spray Equipment as Plants Grow
Coverage declines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Coverage declines.

items.text: Error Thirteen: Ignoring the Harvest Programme
The selected material may conflict with the preharvest interval.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The selected material may conflict with the preharvest interval.

items.text: Error Fourteen: Failing to Update the Programme Weekly
Risk level can change rapidly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Risk level can change rapidly.

items.text: Error Fifteen: Evaluating Success by Number of Sprays Alone
The final criteria are accepted yield and sustainability.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The final criteria are accepted yield and sustainability.

items.text: Research Priorities Required in Egypt

items: 1. Establish a national Tuta monitoring network.
4. Link traps with temperature and plant inspection.
7. Study the role of potato and eggplant fields.
8. Study moth movement among production cycles.
10. Test microbial strains under Egyptian temperatures.
15. Measure the effect of terminating old fields.
16. Study the effect of plant density and canopy cover.
18. Link factory programmes to area-wide management.
20. Evaluate economic viability across several seasons.

[[prepared]]4. Link traps with temperature and plant inspection.
[[prepared]]7. Study the role of potato and eggplant fields.
[[prepared]]8. Study moth movement among production cycles.
[[prepared]]10. Test microbial strains under Egyptian temperatures.

items.text: General Executive Recommendations
The following recommendations apply across Egyptian programmes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following recommendations apply across Egyptian programmes:

items: Do not repeat an IRAC group.
Terminate the field when economic viability ends.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Terminate the field when economic viability ends.

items.text: Scientific Summary of Section Eighteen

No single programme is suitable for all tomato regions and production cycles in Egypt because the development rate of Tuta absoluta, number of generations, immigration sources, and crop stage differ among regions and seasons.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single programme is suitable for all tomato regions and production cycles in Egypt because the development rate of Tuta absoluta, number of generations, immigration sources, and crop stage differ among regions and seasons.

items.text: Egyptian programmes should be built by integrating:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Egyptian programmes should be built by integrating:

items.text: Processing tomatoes require protection of canopy cover and fruits, review of factory requirements, and immediate field termination after delivery ends.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Processing tomatoes require protection of canopy cover and fruits, review of factory requirements, and immediate field termination after delivery ends.

items.text: Fresh-market tomatoes require frequent fruit inspection, precise management of preharvest intervals between pickings, and organized handling of crates and rejected fruits.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Fresh-market tomatoes require frequent fruit inspection, precise management of preharvest intervals between pickings, and organized handling of crates and rejected fruits.

items.text: During winter, pest development may slow but does not stop, and long crop duration may permit several generations.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]During winter, pest development may slow but does not stop, and long crop duration may permit several generations.

items.text: Spring is characterized by the risk of faster development and moth movement from old winter fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Spring is characterized by the risk of faster development and moth movement from old winter fields.

items.text: Summer requires closer inspection and faster response to eggs and young larvae, together with protection of biological agents from heat, dryness, and radiation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Summer requires closer inspection and faster response to eggs and young larvae, together with protection of biological agents from heat, dryness, and radiation.

items.text: Autumn may begin under high pressure resulting from accumulated summer generations and intensive insecticide-use history.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Autumn may begin under high pressure resulting from accumulated summer generations and intensive insecticide-use history.

items.text: In the Delta, priorities include coordination among holdings and management of drainage canals, nurseries, and old fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In the Delta, priorities include coordination among holdings and management of drainage canals, nurseries, and old fields.

items.text: In Nubaria and Wadi El Natroun, priorities include dividing large areas into sectors, using maps, and organizing harvesting and residue management.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In Nubaria and Wadi El Natroun, priorities include dividing large areas into sectors, using maps, and organizing harvesting and residue management.

items.text: On the North Coast and in Matrouh, data should be interpreted according to local climate and wind.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]On the North Coast and in Matrouh, data should be interpreted according to local climate and wind.

items.text: In Upper Egypt, rapid pest development requires shorter intervals between inspection and decision, together with protection of biological agents from heat and dryness.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]In Upper Egypt, rapid pest development requires shorter intervals between inspection and decision, together with protection of biological agents from heat and dryness.

items.text: Short-cycle crops require prevention from the transplant stage, whereas long-cycle crops require intensive resistance-management and biological-control systems.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Short-cycle crops require prevention from the transplant stage, whereas long-cycle crops require intensive resistance-management and biological-control systems.

items.text: An Egyptian programme should not contain a fixed spray schedule, but a weekly system that reviews:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An Egyptian programme should not contain a fixed spray schedule, but a weekly system that reviews:

items.text: Programmes become sustainably applicable when they move from general recommendations to documented local protocols whose results and costs are reviewed at the end of every season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Programmes become sustainably applicable when they move from general recommendations to documented local protocols whose results and costs are reviewed at the end of every season.
